title: juncus marginatus
intro: Juncus marginatus, also known as creeping rush, is a type of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its roots and leaves. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and soothing respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, creeping rush is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
